title: The One With The Kips

jokes.text: That's because you always sleep till noon, silly.
jokes.setup: Character confused about why it's dark at 9am
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals the obvious reason with condescending sweetness
jokes.explanation: Simple character-based humor that establishes Monica's slightly controlling nature while gently mocking the other person's sleep habits

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why is it so dark? It's only 9 in the morning.

dialogue.speaker: Someone
dialogue.line: Monica, it's 9 at night.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: This is what nine looks like.
jokes.setup: Continued confusion about morning darkness
jokes.punchline: Monica delivers matter-of-fact explanation like teaching a child
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character voice - Monica's maternal condescension perfectly captured in five words

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's called winter. The sun comes up later. It's science.

jokes.text: All weekend? That's a whole lot of naked.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests being naked the entire weekend
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mathematically concerned response
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - turning romantic suggestion into awkward mathematical assessment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. The entire weekend? That's like 60 hours!

jokes.text: That's funny. Seems like Chandler's conference could've been... in Connecticut or Vermont.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler both claim to have events in Jersey
jokes.punchline: Phoebe innocently suggests alternative locations, exposing the lie
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection that creates suspense about the secret relationship while showcasing Phoebe's naive perceptiveness

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Why don't you guys just go to Connecticut instead? Or Delaware? I hear Delaware's nice.

jokes.text: Do you want people to think it's fake? It's a real conference.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's innocent suggestion threatens their cover story
jokes.punchline: Chandler overcompensates with desperate insistence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's panic when caught in a lie, typical of his neurotic tendencies

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're not a couple! We're just two guys who live together, share a bed, and enjoy romantic dinners by candlelight. Perfectly normal!

jokes.text: I'm focusing on the 'I get to see my wife' part... and not on the part that makes me do this.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining he can't see Rachel when Emily visits
jokes.punchline: Ross does some physical gesture of distress while speaking
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment implied by dialogue structure - Ross's visible distress about the situation

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I can't see Rachel when Emily's in town. I just... I can't.

jokes.text: So I'm thinking, basically, we pick it up and then we flip it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about flipping Monica's mattress
jokes.punchline: Chandler states the obvious process as if it's profound strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment - treating simple task like complex problem-solving with mock seriousness

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You flip it over, then you flip it back.

jokes.text: That's better than my way.
jokes.setup: Chandler's obvious mattress-flipping strategy
jokes.punchline: Joey genuinely impressed by the basic logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent character beat showing Joey's simple nature - he's genuinely amazed by common sense

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I flipped my mattress.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, that's a good idea. How come you thought of that?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, you flip your mattress to even out the wear.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! Oh! That's genius!

jokes.text: Hey, Joey's ass.
jokes.setup: Joey apparently bent over or positioned awkwardly during mattress flipping
jokes.punchline: Phoebe casually greets his exposed posterior
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's matter-of-fact delivery of absurd greeting creates perfect visual comedy moment with economy

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Hey Joey.

jokes.text: When Ross said 'Rachel' at the wedding, I figured it'd happen again.
jokes.setup: Chandler explains why he prepared emergency supplies
jokes.punchline: References Ross's wedding disaster as predictor of future drama
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Chandler learning from past group disasters and preparing accordingly

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Remember Ross's wedding? That disaster taught me something important. If something that catastrophic can happen at a wedding, who knows what else could go wrong? So I've prepared emergency supplies for every possible scenario.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, that was years ago.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Exactly! Which means we're overdue for the next disaster. I'm not taking chances anymore.

jokes.text: Condoms?
jokes.setup: Chandler listing emergency supplies for being trapped
jokes.punchline: Phoebe discovers unexpected item in survival kit
jokes.explanation: Setup for the repopulation joke - works as surprise reveal but fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, I've got a flashlight, batteries, a first aid kit, some water, and granola bars.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What's this?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's a flare gun.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you're gonna shoot me!

jokes.text: You don't know how long we'll be in here. We may have to repopulate the Earth.
jokes.setup: Question about condoms in emergency kit
jokes.punchline: Chandler escalates to apocalyptic scenario
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from apartment drama to species survival, delivered with Chandler's characteristic over-the-top logic

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, what if there's a nuclear holocaust? I'm not gonna have sex in a nuclear holocaust!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And even if I do, I'm not gonna want to repopulate the Earth. That's not a pretty picture, I don't want to spend the rest of my life looking at my son and thinking, 'Nope, should've used a condom on that one.'

jokes.text: And condoms are the way to do that?
jokes.setup: Chandler's repopulation scenario
jokes.punchline: Phoebe points out logical flaw with innocent confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - innocent logical question that deflates Chandler's absurd premise. Excellent structure and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, if everyone on Earth died, how would the people repopulate?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, theoretically the last man and woman would—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No no no, but like, if EVERYONE died, then there'd be no one left to, you know, do it with.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Our dog died!
jokes.setup: Ross starting to explain his situation with Emily
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dramatic interruption about pet death
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that derails serious conversation with unexpected family tragedy

jokes.text: LaPooh's still alive?
jokes.setup: Rachel's announcement that LaPooh died
jokes.punchline: Ross's surprised question implying dog should have died long ago
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - three words that imply the dog was ancient and overdue for death

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: LaPooh died? How is that possible? She's only been alive for like three weeks!

jokes.text: It says he was hit by an ice cream truck and dragged for... nineteen blocks!
jokes.setup: Learning about LaPooh's death
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and specific details of the accident
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalated by specificity - ice cream truck adds ironic sweetness to tragedy, nineteen blocks is absurdly precise

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, what happened to LaPooh?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: He was hit by a bus.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh no! Which bus?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The M15.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Where was he crossing?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: At the corner of 5th and Madison.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What time of day?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Quarter past three on a Tuesday.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Was he wearing his lucky sweater?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The blue one? With the little poodle on it? Yeah.

jokes.text: You should live with Joey. Rolos everywhere.
jokes.setup: Monica impressed by chocolates on hotel pillows
jokes.punchline: Comparison to Joey's candy-scattered living situation
jokes.explanation: Good character reference that paints vivid picture of Joey's messy candy-filled lifestyle

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, look at these chocolates on the pillow! This is so fancy!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? My apartment looks like this all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, that's not chocolate, that's... I don't even want to know.

jokes.text: Chandler, stop yelling that you and I are gonna do it!
jokes.setup: Chandler apparently being loud about their romantic weekend
jokes.punchline: Monica's embarrassed demand for discretion
jokes.explanation: Classic embarrassment humor - Monica trying to maintain dignity while Chandler lacks filter

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey everyone! Monica and I are going away this weekend!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler! Do you have to announce it to the entire world?

jokes.text: Oh, dear God! They gave us glasses!
jokes.setup: Monica complaining about lipstick on glasses
jokes.punchline: Escalating horror at basic hotel amenities being unsanitary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica germaphobia moment - treating basic hotel glasses like contamination

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, there's lipstick on this glass! This is disgusting!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, it's a hotel glass.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Exactly! Do you know how many people have used this glass? How many mouths have touched this rim?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Mon, you're staying in a nice hotel.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't care! I looked at the remote control and there's a stain on it I can't identify. A STAIN, Rachel!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Did you check the bedspread?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh God, I'm not even going to look at the bedspread. I'm sleeping in my coat.

jokes.text: Okay, but let's do it now. Chopper Five just lost its feed!
jokes.setup: Monica wants to change rooms during romantic weekend
jokes.punchline: Chandler prioritizes TV helicopter coverage over romance
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Chandler's misplaced priorities - urgent about TV coverage while girlfriend wants room change

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We could change rooms.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Change rooms? Monica, there's helicopter coverage!

jokes.text: Oh, not again. This happened when my grandfather died.
jokes.setup: Rachel gets nosebleed during serious conversation
jokes.punchline: Reveals this is recurring response to bad news
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that creates pattern of absurd physical reactions to emotional stress

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that—am I bleeding?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: This always happens! Every time I hear something bad, I just start bleeding from my nose.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's like my body's way of saying 'nope, can't handle this information.'

jokes.text: I know, it's ridiculous. I can't see you, either. All right.
jokes.setup: Ross tells Rachel he can't see her anymore
jokes.punchline: Rachel misunderstands due to nosebleed blocking her vision
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - dramatic relationship moment deflated by literal misunderstanding due to physical comedy

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, I can't see you anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I just... I can't do this.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is it because of my nosebleed? Because I can tilt my head back!

jokes.text: Half a tank? Still lots of high-speed chasing to do!
jokes.setup: Discussion of car chase suspect running low on gas
jokes.punchline: Chandler's optimistic assessment of remaining chase potential
jokes.explanation: Shows Chandler's enthusiasm for TV drama while Monica wants to focus on their relationship

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: He's still got a quarter tank. We could chase him for like... two more hours.

jokes.text: No, like feet.
jokes.setup: Hotel clerk suggests carpet smells like ocean
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact correction to something less pleasant
jokes.explanation: Good economy and timing - deflates hotel employee's attempt to spin bad smell positively

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's not ocean. It's urine.

jokes.text: I hear you, Mugsy.
jokes.setup: Monica aggressively negotiating with hotel staff
jokes.punchline: Chandler treating her like gangster with period-appropriate nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect nickname for Monica's aggressive behavior, delivered with Chandler's characteristic deflating humor

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Easy there, Scarface.

jokes.text: Do not speak ill of the dead.
jokes.setup: Monica criticizing the car chase suspect's poor planning
jokes.punchline: Chandler defending fictional criminal with funeral solemnity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - treating TV criminal with reverence usually reserved for actual deceased people

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I mean, how stupid do you have to be? You're on a high-speed chase and you think you can lose the cops by driving through a residential neighborhood?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, maybe he had a very good reason. Maybe his grandmother just died and he was rushing to the funeral.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, he's a bank robber!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A grieving bank robber.

jokes.text: What did you say? / I said, 'Jeez, relax, Mon.'
jokes.setup: Chandler calls Monica 'Mom' during argument
jokes.punchline: Chandler's unconvincing cover-up of Freudian slip
jokes.explanation: Classic relationship comedy - Freudian slip reveals unconscious feelings, poorly covered up

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I mean... Mom... nica. Monica. I said Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Did you just call me Mom?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No! No, I said 'Mo-om-nica.' It's a new thing I'm doing with your name. Monica. With emphasis.

jokes.text: I don't like fast getaways, I like car chases.
jokes.setup: Monica storms out after calling him out
jokes.punchline: Chandler's pedantic correction while being dumped
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment - missing emotional point to make literal correction about his TV preferences

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm done. I can't do this anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, technically, you can do it anymore. 'Anymore' means you could continue doing it in the future, but you're choosing not to. If you couldn't do it, you'd say you can't do it 'any longer.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm leaving!

jokes.text: Well, Davey's still pining after LaPooh... but, you know, he's a tough little turtle.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about other family pets after LaPooh's death
jokes.punchline: Rachel describes turtle's grief like a war veteran
jokes.explanation: Good anthropomorphization of turtle emotions, treating pet grief with human psychological complexity

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, LaPooh was really depressed for a while. He just sat there staring at nothing. It was like he'd seen things... terrible things.

jokes.text: Lucky me! Oh, my God! That is good news, Ross! That's the best news I've heard since LaPooh died!
jokes.setup: Ross saying they can still see each other until Emily arrives
jokes.punchline: Rachel's bitter sarcasm comparing to recent pet death
jokes.explanation: Strong sarcastic escalation that callbacks to pet death while expressing rage at Ross's selfishness

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh sure, we can see each other. Like I could see Mr. Whiskers after he died and they put him in that little box.

jokes.text: Yeah? Well, that's how mad I am!
jokes.setup: Rachel storming out of her own apartment
jokes.punchline: Defiant justification of logically impossible action
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - anger so great it defies spatial logic. Strong quotable line with escalating absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm leaving and I'm taking my stuff with me!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, this is my apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't care! I'm leaving anyway!

jokes.text: Damn Rolos!
jokes.setup: Joey returning from somewhere, apparently frustrated
jokes.punchline: Blaming candy for some unseen mishap
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Joey's candy-filled lifestyle, suggesting Rolos caused some off-screen problem

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't believe this! That candy bar ruined everything!

jokes.text: You know, at the Taj Mahal he has his own private glass elevator.
jokes.setup: Discussing seeing Donald Trump at hotel
jokes.punchline: Joey's random but specific Trump trivia
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey random knowledge moment - oddly specific trivia about celebrity elevators

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I saw Donald Trump at the hotel today.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Did you know that Donald Trump's middle name is John? Donald John Trump. I don't know why I know that.

jokes.text: What kinds of stuff do you know?
jokes.setup: Joey's random Trump elevator knowledge
jokes.punchline: Chandler's amazed question about Joey's trivia collection
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that highlights the randomness of Joey's knowledge while showing Chandler's bewilderment

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Did you know that Donald Trump's apartment building has an elevator with gold plating?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How do you even KNOW that?

jokes.text: Maybe the food tasted good at first... but then made everybody vomit and have diarrhea.
jokes.setup: Monica saying some people don't appreciate good food
jokes.punchline: Joey's graphic alternative explanation for the 'awful' culinary fair
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey gross-out humor, escalating from taste complaints to food poisoning scenario

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Some people just don't appreciate good food.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, or maybe they just have bad taste buds. I mean, how else do you explain someone not liking pizza? That's like not liking... breathing.

jokes.text: Just think of it as $25 per room.
jokes.setup: Chandler shocked at $300 hotel cost
jokes.punchline: Monica's creative math to justify excessive room-switching
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's attempt to rationalize her compulsive behavior with creative accounting

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Three hundred dollars?! For one night?!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, if you think about it, we switched rooms four times, so that's really only seventy-five dollars per room.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, that's not how math works.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It works if you don't think about it too hard.

jokes.text: Chandler stole a 20 from my purse!
jokes.setup: Friends asking what they're arguing about
jokes.punchline: Monica's dramatic accusation over petty money dispute
jokes.explanation: Relationship comedy showing how couples fight over trivial things while hiding bigger issues

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He borrowed twenty dollars from me three months ago and never paid it back!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, I was going to pay you back!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Were you? WERE YOU? Because it seems like you've just decided to steal from me!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh come on, it's twenty dollars!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's not about the money, Chandler. It's about trust. It's about respect. It's about the fact that you clearly don't care about our friendship!

jokes.text: You should've seen the look on her face.
jokes.setup: Ross describing telling Rachel about Emily's demand
jokes.punchline: Implied visual of Rachel's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for unseen visual comedy - audience imagines Rachel's devastating reaction

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I told Rachel that Emily said I had to choose between her and Rachel, and Rachel just stood there with this look of complete horror on her face.

jokes.text: You got married too fast. / That's not advice.
jokes.setup: Ross asking for Joey's advice about his marriage problems
jokes.punchline: Joey states obvious problem, Ross rejects it as unhelpful
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Joey's simple wisdom vs Ross's need for complex solutions to avoid responsibility

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Your wife doesn't like you.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not helpful, Joey.

jokes.text: Maybe I could do it.
jokes.setup: Everyone agreeing they couldn't give up any friend
jokes.punchline: Phoebe casually considering abandoning someone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - casual consideration of something others find unthinkable, delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I could give up Chandler.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! It's happening. It's already started. I'm Kip.
jokes.setup: Seeing Ross in the coffee shop and panicking
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dramatic realization she's becoming the phased-out friend
jokes.explanation: Strong moment of paranoid realization - Rachel seeing her worst fear coming true in real time

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that Ross? What is he doing here? He always comes on Thursdays!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait... what day is it today?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's Thursday.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh God. Oh God, no. He didn't even call me. He just... came here without telling me.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm becoming the phased-out friend. I'm Chandler's girlfriend's sister!

jokes.text: Who's Kip?
jokes.setup: Rachel's dramatic declaration about becoming Kip
jokes.punchline: Joey's innocent question proves Rachel's point about being forgotten
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic validation of Rachel's fears - Joey's ignorance perfectly demonstrates how people get erased from group memory

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm moving to Paris. I'm going to become Kip.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Who's Kip?

jokes.text: Oh, that poor bastard.
jokes.setup: Learning about Kip being phased out of the group
jokes.punchline: Joey's belated sympathy for someone he just learned existed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - immediate emotional investment in someone he just learned about moments ago

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, who's Kip?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh man, that's terrible. Poor guy.

jokes.text: Kip didn't even have to be Kip. We handled that all wrong.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how Kip got phased out
jokes.punchline: Chandler's guilt about their past friend-elimination process
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on sitcom group dynamics and how friendships get written out of shows

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, we all agreed we'd phase him out gradually. You know, stop inviting him to things, take longer to return his calls...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: But I feel terrible about it. I mean, the guy was always nice to us.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We basically ghosted him before ghosting was even a thing.

jokes.text: Chandler had a mustache, for crying out loud.
jokes.setup: Explaining how long ago and different things were
jokes.punchline: Chandler's past facial hair as evidence of ancient history
jokes.explanation: Great use of embarrassing character detail as time marker - suggests mustache was so bad it marks an era

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That was so long ago! Things were completely different back then.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How different?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You had a goatee!

jokes.text: I just assumed Phoebe would be the one to go. You live far away, you're not related. You lift right out.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining her fears about being phased out
jokes.punchline: Casual analysis of Phoebe's expendability with business-like efficiency
jokes.explanation: Brutally logical friendship triage delivered with corporate efficiency - 'lift right out' is perfect phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm worried that I'm going to be phased out. Like, what if I'm not essential anymore?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, please. You're totally essential.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, I mean, we all are. We're a unit.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Actually, if we're being honest, Phoebe's pretty expendable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? She sings to herself. She makes up her own words. Nobody even knows what she does for a living.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm a masseuse!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: See? Nobody believed you.

jokes.text: Yes, that was mine.
jokes.setup: Hotel calling about eyelash curler left in Chandler's room
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact admission of using feminine beauty tool
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Chandler's nonchalant admission creates more questions than it answers

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, that's mine. I use it. Problem?

jokes.text: I don't even feel like I know you anymore.
jokes.setup: Learning Chandler uses an eyelash curler
jokes.punchline: Joey's dramatic existential crisis over beauty tool revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey overreaction - treating minor revelation like a fundamental betrayal of their friendship

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Chandler uses an eyelash curler? An eyelash curler?! What is happening to the world? What's next, he's gonna tell me he uses a deep conditioning mask? That he plucks his eyebrows? I mean, come on, man, where does it end? When did taking care of yourself become so... so complicated?

jokes.text: Were you or were you not... on a gay cruise?
jokes.setup: Joey's growing suspicion about Chandler's secret weekend
jokes.punchline: Joey's wildly incorrect conclusion about the conference
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from eyelash curler to completely wrong conclusion - shows Joey's simple logic chain

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, where do you go every weekend? You're always sneaking off somewhere.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not sneaking off. I told you, I have a conference.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A conference? Yeah, right. I know what's really going on.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You're training to be a secret agent! Or wait—you're a spy! No, no, no... you're building a robot in a warehouse somewhere!

jokes.text: Bye, Ross. Forever.
jokes.setup: Phoebe leaving Rachel and Ross to talk privately
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic farewell treating temporary exit like permanent goodbye
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - treating mundane social exit with melodramatic finality, excellent economy

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm gonna go. I'm gonna leave you two alone. And if I never see you again, I want you to know that you meant the world to me.

jokes.text: First, I'd have said the right name at my wedding.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what Rachel would do in his situation
jokes.punchline: Rachel's brutal callback to Ross's wedding mistake
jokes.explanation: Devastating callback delivered with perfect timing and economy - cuts to the heart of Ross's responsibility

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't know, Ross. I mean, what would you do? Oh wait, I know — you'd say the wrong name at the altar!

jokes.text: This isn't over?
jokes.setup: Monica explaining that fights don't end relationships
jokes.punchline: Chandler's amazed confusion about relationship dynamics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's inexperience with healthy relationships and conflict resolution

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're saying fights don't end relationships?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because I've been doing this all wrong. I thought one argument and you're supposed to break up.

jokes.text: Welcome to an adult relationship.
jokes.setup: Chandler learning that fights don't end relationships
jokes.punchline: Monica's maternal guidance about relationship maturity
jokes.explanation: Solid relationship humor about maturity levels and learning to handle conflict constructively

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, so you and Rachel had a fight. That doesn't mean anything is wrong with your relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Grown-ups fight. It's normal. It's healthy, even.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What matters is that you talk about it, you work through it, and you come out the other side stronger.

jokes.text: I'm afraid so.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if they're in a relationship
jokes.punchline: Monica's mock-reluctant confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery treating relationship status like an unfortunate diagnosis

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Are we in a relationship?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I guess so.

jokes.text: It is. It hurts.
jokes.setup: Monica saying keeping the secret is hard
jokes.punchline: Joey's physical description of information-withholding
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment treating emotional difficulty as physical pain - very characteristic response

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's like, you know, I got this thing inside me, and I gotta keep it in. It's like I'm holding in a fart at a fancy dinner.

jokes.text: We're so stupid. Do you know what's going on in there? They're trying to take Joey!
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler realizing Joey knows their secret
jokes.punchline: Chandler's paranoid interpretation of normal conversation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chandler's paranoia about friend loyalty, treating Joey like contested territory

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey knows.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? No, he doesn't!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He does. He figured it out.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How could he figure it out? We've been so careful!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, you guys want some gum?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: GUM?! Why would he offer us gum? Is that code? Did he mean something else by gum? Oh God, he knows everything!

jokes.text: 'The most popular Phoebe in tennis is called the overhand Phoebe. If you win, slap your opponent on the Phoebe and say, Hi, Phoebe!'
jokes.setup: Joey reading Mad Libs where they used Phoebe for every blank
jokes.punchline: Absurd tennis instructions with Phoebe's name inappropriately substituted
jokes.explanation: Standard Mad Libs humor - absurd results from inappropriate word substitution, mildly amusing but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, here we go. 'To play tennis, first you need a Phoebe.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: 'Then you need a Phoebe and a Phoebe.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: 'You hit the Phoebe over the Phoebe, and your opponent tries to Phoebe it back.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: 'If the Phoebe lands outside the Phoebe, it's a Phoebe.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: 'The first player to win six Phoebes wins the Phoebe.'

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm a sport now?

jokes.text: Rules are good! Rules help control the fun.
jokes.setup: Everyone leaving while Monica wants to follow Mad Libs rules
jokes.punchline: Monica's desperate defense of structure over spontaneity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica line - her need to control everything, even party games. 'Control the fun' is brilliantly contradictory phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait! You can't just leave! We haven't finished the Mad Libs properly!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, it's just a game.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's just a game?! It's a SYSTEM! You need an adjective, then a noun, then a verb! There's an ORDER to these things!

tmdb_overview: Monica and Chandler plan an Atlantic City getaway in order to have an intimate weekend away from their friends.  Meanwhile, Ross prepares himself to tell Rachel that he can't see her anymore.
insight: Steady character work carries a middle-tier episode through observational humor without standout moments.
